content.problemStatement:
You are given an array of integers. Your task is to traverse the array using a pointer and decrement the pointer to access the previous elements of the array.


Input Format
The first line contains an integer `n`, representing the size of the array.

The second line contains `n` space-separated integers, representing the elements of the array.



Output Format
Print the elements of the array in reverse order, each element on a new line, by decrementing the pointer from the last element to the first.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 15), the size of the array. The second line contains n space‑separated integers arr[i] (1 ≤ arr[i] ≤ 100).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the n elements of the given array in reverse order, each on its own line. The outputs of consecutive test cases follow one another without blank lines.

sampleTestCases.explanation: One test case with n=5. The array is printed from the last element to the first.
Single element array; reversing does not change order.

solution.approach: The task is simply to output the array in reverse order. For each test case we read the array, then iterate from the last index down to zero and print each element. No additional data structures are required; we can either use an index loop or a pointer that starts at the last element and is decremented. The algorithm runs in O(n) time per test case and uses O(1) extra space besides the input array.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case, O(total N) for the whole input
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing the current array (n ≤ 15)
